allowed_pairs.reason: Product family overlap is expected; coral queries sources while coral-create-source-spec writes manifests.
Product family overlap is expected; coral queries sources while coral-review-source-spec reviews manifests.
Both concern Coral source specs, but one creates manifests and the other reviews them.
community-space-ops covers full venue/community operations; venue-event-calendar only builds the programming calendar.
developer-email-writer handles one-off email/newsletter copy; devrel-email-sequence builds a multi-email drip.
event-brief-generator includes a single event blast; devrel-email-sequence handles ongoing drip campaigns.
client-delivery-copilot creates project delivery plans; meeting-to-momentum extracts decisions and action items.
hackathon-in-a-box plans an entire event; hackathon-rubric-builder focuses on judging and scoring.
content-engine-builder is audience-agnostic; devrel-content-multiplier is specific to developer launches and demos.
agentic-engineering is a build reference; agentic-engineering-audit scores an existing agent system.
agent-opportunity-audit finds AI opportunities; business-workflow-decoder maps existing operations.
nebius is broad cloud operations; agent-deploy-kubeclaw is a specific Kubernetes deployment workflow.
nebius is broad cloud operations; nebius-token-factory-integration only wires LLM tools to Token Factory.
sponsor-marketplace-matcher finds target sponsors; sponsor-pitch-deck creates the pitch asset.
sponsor-marketplace-matcher produces the target list; sponsor-outreach-kit builds the sponsorship package and outreach.
event-recap-report is post-event proof and renewal material; sponsor-pitch-deck is pre-event sales collateral.
event-recap-report summarizes completed events; sponsor-marketplace-matcher identifies prospective sponsors before outreach.
event-recap-report creates post-event reporting; hackathon-in-a-box plans the full hackathon.
multi-event-radar ranks external event opportunities; venue-event-calendar plans programming for one physical venue.
hackathon-in-a-box is planning; hackathon-orchestration manages live event operations.
multi-event-radar decides where to attend or sponsor; sponsor-marketplace-matcher decides which sponsors to approach.
event-recap-report is a proof artifact after an event; sponsor-outreach-kit prepares the sales package before outreach.
devrel-email-sequence writes developer-facing drips; sponsor-outreach-kit writes sponsor-facing sales outreach.
hackathon-orchestration handles live logistics; hackathon-rubric-builder creates judging criteria and scoring.
event-recap-report looks backward at a finished event; multi-event-radar ranks future event opportunities.

Intentional: event-leads is the broad one-off enrichment pipeline (web-verify everyone, social-influence ranking, builder/judge curation, gated dashboard); event-lead-ranker is the config-driven, repeatable per-sponsor scoring plus the operations workflow (who to APPROVE to hit a headcount, which GOING leads to bump). They compose.

Surface overlap only (both mention sponsors): sponsor-marketplace-matcher finds which sponsors to approach for an event; event-lead-ranker scores event attendees as leads for already-secured sponsors. Different inputs and outputs.

Likely merge or router candidate: both rank events, with multi-event-radar scoped to city/region planning.
Likely boundary cleanup: event-radar includes lead scoring while event-leads handles CSV-based lead ranking.
